bug_report.summary: Clicking in the editor results in Zettlr leaving maximized split-view state and appearing partially off-screen (desktop visible)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch Zettlr with the main window maximized and split view visible.
(Optional, as in scenario) Open a menu (e.g., Edit).
Click back into the editor area to close the menu and continue editing.
expected_behaviors: The menu closes and the user can continue typing in the same editor pane.
The main window remains maximized and fully on-screen; split-view layout remains intact.
actual_behaviors: The desktop becomes visible and Zettlr is no longer maximized, appearing shifted to the lower-right and partially off-screen.
The editor layout/state changes unexpectedly, disrupting the editing workflow.

bug_report.summary: Typing into the new document instead edits 'Welcome to Zettlr!' (wrong document receives input; tab becomes dirty)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch Zettlr and create a new empty Markdown document (timestamp-named file).
Attempt to focus the editor for the new document and type text (e.g., 'Alpha line').
expected_behaviors: Text is inserted into the newly created blank document.
The tab/title for the newly created document becomes dirty (if unsaved), not an unrelated existing document.
actual_behaviors: The active tab shows '* Welcome to Zettlr!' and the typed text appears there, indicating the Welcome tutorial document received the input instead of the new blank document.

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch

bug_report.summary: Insert Image produces extra stray parentheses '()' around/after the image reference
steps_to_reproduce: Place the caret between the two spaces.
expected_behaviors: Only a standard Markdown image reference should be inserted (e.g., `![](/tmp/insert-image-1x1.png)`), without additional unrelated characters.
actual_behaviors: The inserted content includes extra `()` after the image reference and another `()` on the following line, cluttering the document and contributing to the inline-layout break.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Markdown image still renders as a block widget instead of inline for `X ![alt](...) Y`
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new empty Markdown document.
Ensure the editor is in preview/WYSIWYG rendering mode (Rendering: Preview).
expected_behaviors: The image renders as an inline widget/placeholder within the same line, between `X` and `Y` (no forced line breaks around the image).
The line remains a single paragraph/line (and, when invisibles are shown, only one pilcrow appears at the end of the line).
actual_behaviors: The image renders as a large block widget/placeholder, pushing `X` above and `Y` below the image (i.e., the content is broken into multiple lines/blocks).

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
